description: To 'someru' is to attend to another participant's work under a claimed responsibility. Supervision may include answering questions and checking conditions within an agreed scope. 'somela' (observe) attends without that responsibility; supervision adds no automatic authority.
articulatory_notes: Make unvoiced 's' through the alveolar groove and round into true-mid back 'o'. Voiced 'm' closes the lips and sends the sound through the nose before stressed true-mid front 'e'. Turn the tongue tip in one light tap or brief trill, then deepen into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'so' narrows into the closed lips of stressed 'me', then final 'ru' lets the tongue turn and the vowel deepen. The held middle and outward release suggest attention kept on work before a response is made.
usage_notes: The supervised work, task, or participant is the object. Its event noun is supervision. Scope and claimed responsibility belong near the verb when they are disputed. Coordination, facilitation, observation, and technical control remain separate relations.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A bounded supervision word keeps attention to one work relation from expanding silently into authority over a whole person.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Close guidance can keep a learner safe and make pressure, posture, or timing perceptible while the practical relationship remains open to question.

semantic_domains.activity: attention to another participant's work under a claimed responsibility
semantic_domains.cognition: observation used to answer questions or respond to conditions
semantic_domains.community: a bounded work relation whose authority has a separate source
